items.title: GPT-5.4 is here.
items.raw_summary:
GPT-5.4 is here.

Native computer-use capabilities.
Up to 1M tokens of context in Codex and the API.
Best-in-class agentic coding for complex tasks.
Scalable tool search across larger ecosystems.
More efficient reasoning for long, tool-heavy workflows.

https://t.co/xuLt562S9b ht
items.why_it_matters: GPT-5.4 launches with native computer-use, 1M context in API/Codex, and best-in-class agentic coding — OpenAI's most capable frontier model yet.
items.action: Read the GPT-5.4 system card and test API access

items.title: We're publishing a new evaluation suite and research paper on Chain-of-Thought (CoT) Controllability
items.raw_summary:
We're publishing a new evaluation suite and research paper on Chain-of-Thought (CoT) Controllability.

We find that GPT-5.4 Thinking shows low ability to obscure its reasoning—suggesting CoT monitoring remains a useful safety tool. https://t.co/isZkNkPXZm
items.why_it_matters: OpenAI publishes Chain-of-Thought Controllability research showing GPT-5.4 has low ability to obscure reasoning, validating CoT monitoring as a safety tool.
items.action: Review the CoT controllability eval suite for safety implications

items.title: 📦 openai/openai-python v2.25.0
items.raw_summary: ## 2.25.0 (2026-03-05)  Full Changelog: [v2.24.0...v2.25.0](https://github.com/openai/openai-python/compare/v2.24.0...v2.25.0)  ### Features  * **api:** gpt-5.4, tool search tool, and new computer tool ([6b2043f](https://github.com/openai/openai-python/commit/6b2043f3d63058f5582e
items.why_it_matters: OpenAI Python SDK v2.25.0 ships GPT-5.4 support including tool search and the new computer tool, enabling immediate developer adoption.
items.action: Upgrade openai-python and explore new computer tool API

items.title: Codex got more speed.
items.raw_summary:
Codex got more speed.

With /fast mode, GPT-5.4 runs 1.5x faster with the same intelligence and reasoning.

Move through coding tasks, iteration, and debugging while staying in flow. https://t.co/Xlzmeozzc4
items.why_it_matters: Codex /fast mode runs GPT-5.4 at 1.5x speed with same intelligence — a direct productivity multiplier for AI-assisted coding workflows.
items.action: Try /fast mode in Codex for iterative coding tasks

items.title: Relicensing with AI-Assisted Rewrite

items.title: 📦 ggerganov/llama.cpp b8210
items.raw_summary: <details open>  CUDA:  Improve performance via less synchronizations between token (#17795)  * Adds CPU-to-CUDA copy capability to ggml_backend_cuda_cpy_tensor_async()  * Adds function to relax sync requirements between input copies on supported backends (CUDA for now)  * Exchang
items.why_it_matters: llama.cpp b8210 reduces CUDA synchronizations between tokens — direct inference speedup for self-hosted GPU deployments.
items.action: Update llama.cpp for faster CUDA inference

items.title: New course: Build and Train an LLM with JAX, built in partnership with @Google and taught by @chrisa
items.raw_summary:
New course: Build and Train an LLM with JAX, built in partnership with @Google and taught by @chrisachard.

JAX is the open-source library behind Google's Gemini, Veo, and other advanced models. This short course teaches you to build and train a 20-million parameter language model from scratch using JAX and its ecosystem of tools.

You'll implement a complete MiniGPT-style architecture from scratch, train it, and chat with your finished model through a graphical interface.

Skills you'll gain:
-
items.why_it_matters: Andrew Ng and Google partner on a JAX course teaching 20M-parameter LLM training from scratch — learn the framework behind Gemini and Veo.
items.action: Take the JAX course to understand Google's ML stack

items.title: Introducing React Grab
items.raw_summary:
Introducing React Grab

Select any element on your page → tell Claude Code or Codex what to change

Fully open source

npx react-grab@latest https://t.co/QnXMFcBV2F
items.why_it_matters: React Grab lets you visually select any page element and pipe it to Claude Code or Codex — bridges the visual-to-code gap for frontend iteration.
items.action: Try npx react-grab@latest in your React project

items.title: 📦 ollama/ollama v0.17.7

items.why_it_matters: Ollama v0.17.7 loosens thinking level constraints and adds Qwen 3.5 context length support at launch — iterating fast on local LLM experience.

items.title: 🚨 Do you understand what just happened?
items.raw_summary:
🚨 Do you understand what just happened?

New York wants to ban AI from answering questions about medicine, law, and engineering.

Let me translate that for you:

> The industries charging you $500/hour just lobbied to make sure you can't get the same answers for free.

> This was never about safety. It's about protecting the bill rate.

> Lawyers don't want you to know AI can draft a contract in 30 seconds.

> Doctors don't want you to know AI can read your bloodwork better than a resident.

>
items.why_it_matters: New York proposes banning AI from answering medical, legal, and engineering questions — a regulatory precedent that could shape AI product design nationwide.
items.action: Track this bill if you build AI products in regulated domains

items.title: 🚨 🚨 BREAKING: Alibaba just gave the AI agent community a powerful, real-world sandbox tool for fre
items.raw_summary:
🚨 🚨 BREAKING: Alibaba just gave the AI agent community a powerful, real-world sandbox tool for free.

It's called OpenSandbox and it gives any AI agent a secure, isolated environment to execute code, browse the web, and train models with a unified API across languages.

No vendor lock-in. No per-minute billing surprises. No stitching together 5 different tools.

Built on the same infra Alibaba uses internally to run AI workloads at massive scale.

What it actually does:
→ Spins up isolated san
items.why_it_matters: Alibaba open-sources OpenSandbox — secure isolated environments for AI agents to execute code, browse web, and train models via unified API.
items.action: Evaluate OpenSandbox for agent sandboxing needs

items.title: China's Alibaba just dropped a Python framework for building multi-agent apps.
items.raw_summary:
China's Alibaba just dropped a Python framework for building multi-agent apps.

AgentScope lets you build AI agents visually with MCP tools, memory, rag, and reasoning capabilities.

Works with any LLM and supports real-time steering.

100% Opensource. https://t.co/UT9QxjJxah
items.why_it_matters: Alibaba's AgentScope provides visual multi-agent app building with MCP tools, memory, RAG, and reasoning — works with any LLM, fully open-source.
